channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Zombie filler: Dead people's fat is the latest beauty trend
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Japan's newspaper-printed solar cells could turn almost any surface into a power generator
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US judge scraps Trump's IRS settlement deal, says lawsuit sought to 'manipulate judicial process'
item.description: A federal judge voided President Trump's settlement with the IRS. The lawsuit was deemed an improper attempt to manipulate the judicial process. Sanctions and disciplinary actions were recommended against involved lawyers. The judge found the parties were not truly opposing each other. The Justice Department was criticized for its role in the settlement.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Why New Zealand forests look like a galaxy filled with glowworms at night
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 5 lakes with underwater forests: From Clear Lake to Caddo Lake
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Ex-Meta techie wraps up 14 years of life in US after layoff, says he's tired of immigration clock
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Mastermind was not happy': Louvre burglars reveal how they pulled off €88 million jewel heist
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Inside Christopher Plummer’s former Darien waterfront estate with a $12.995 million price tag
item.description: A historic Colonial estate from 1866, previously home to actor Christopher Plummer, has hit the market in Darien, Connecticut. This stunning waterfront property, boasting modern updates and direct access to a private sandy beach, exemplifies luxury coastal living. With a high-demand property market in Darien, interested buyers will need to act quickly to seize the opportunity.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists say crushing deep-ocean pressure squeezes hidden nutrients out of sinking marine snow, feeding microbes and changing what we know about Earth’s carbon cycle
item.description: Scientists have discovered that the immense pressure in the deep ocean squeezes nutrients out of sinking marine snow—tiny particles of dead algae and organic matter—providing an unexpected food source for deep-sea microbes. The findings suggest more carbon escapes into deep ocean water than previously believed, which could reshape scientists' understanding of Earth's carbon cycle and improve future climate models.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: When will life on Earth come to an end? Scientists have finally found the doomsday date
item.description: Earth's plant life may persist for approximately 1.8 billion more years. The Sun's increasing brightness will gradually make conditions too harsh for vegetation. Hardy plant species adapted to extreme conditions will likely be the last survivors. This slow fading of life contrasts with dramatic disaster movie endings. The planet's eventual uninhabitability will unfold over an immense stretch of time.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A US inmate demanded pasta in Tihar: What does a prison meal look like in different countries
item.description: A US citizen arrested in a terror case seeks court permission to cook his own food. He has been on a hunger strike for over 50 days due to prison food. Matthew Aaron VanDyke claims jail meals are unsuitable for his dietary habits. His family is willing to cover all expenses for his personal cooking setup. The court has sought a response from Tihar Jail authorities.
item.dc:creator: TOI Trending Desk

item.title: 'More destructive than US': China hits out at Japan's military role in South China Sea
item.dc:creator: TOI defence desk

item.title: 'Intolerable threat to US sovereignty': Why Trump wants to 'dismantle' International Criminal Court
item.description: The Trump administration initiated a campaign against the International Criminal Court. This action targets the court's authority over American officials and military personnel. The United States argues the court threatens national sovereignty and self-governance. Diplomatic efforts and potential sanctions will be employed to disable the court. This move aims to protect American citizens from international prosecution.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'King' Guramrit Sidhu sentenced to 20 years in prison in Los Angeles for US-Canada drug trafficking; spared from life sentence as lawyer says his offence was not violent
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Muhammad Ali, "The Greatest": "I try not to speak about all the charities and people I help, because…" - a timeless lesson reveals that the purest acts of kindness are often the ones no one ever sees
item.description: Muhammad Ali believed true generosity requires no expectation of return. He deliberately avoided publicizing his extensive charitable work and humanitarian efforts. This approach highlighted his commitment to helping others without seeking personal recognition. The article emphasizes that quiet acts of kindness often carry the most significant impact. Ali's perspective remains relevant in today's highly visible world.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: This supersonic jet needed a radiation meter because it flew twice as high as commercial aircraft
item.description: The Concorde jet soared near the edge of space, requiring careful monitoring of radiation levels to ensure passenger safety. In the face of hazardous cosmic storms, pilots were mandated to descend to safer altitudes. While this supersonic aircraft typically flew above the majority of Earth's atmospheric shield, fluctuations in radiation exposure depended on several factors including altitude, geography, and solar activity. During such storms, safety took precedence over speed.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Poland's 300-year-old 'Vampire Cemetery' mystery: The strange reason 100 people were buried with sickles, locks and unusual rituals
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Britain bought 1,525 acres nearly lifeless farmland, now beavers are helping it make money
item.description: A farm in Lincolnshire is evolving into a lucrative wildland endeavor, with beavers playing a pivotal role in this ecological project. The initiative focuses on demonstrating that restoring nature can lead to substantial private income. Current revenue streams are generated through carbon removal and biodiversity credits, with aspirations to draw significant investments for sustainable environmental solutions.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK bans Iran Revolutionary Guards, blames IRGC-backed group for attacks on Jews
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A supernova lit up Earth’s sky in 1054 and was visible in daylight. Hubble now shows its glowing remains are still expanding nearly 1,000 years later
item.description: Astronomers used the Hubble Space Telescope to measure the expanding Crab Nebula. New images compared with older ones show gas filaments moving outwards. This ancient stellar explosion's remnant is still actively changing today. The Crab Pulsar at the center continues to power the nebula's glow. Scientists also identified previously unrecognised filament structures within the nebula.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
